meta.title: Wide Social Media
meta.description: Full-width social media section with heading on the left and icon+label links on the right, separated by a top border.

childTsx: import { useBlockProps, BlockControls, InspectorControls, MediaUpload, MediaReplaceFlow, __experimentalLinkControl as LinkControl } from '@wordpress/block-editor';
import { ToolbarGroup, ToolbarButton, Popover, PanelBody, BaseControl, Button, ToggleControl, TextControl } from '@wordpress/components';
import { image as imageIcon, link as linkIcon, trash } from '@wordpress/icons';

requiredPatterns.usage: Show heading area in editor when block selected or has content
Sync list view name with heading text (parent) and link title (child)
Add new social media items to parent
Render SVG icons inline for currentColor inheritance
Placeholder for icon upload when no logo set
Get media attachment data for logo
Custom link editing popover for icon-only items
PHP: render inline SVG or img for social icons

parent.heading: h2 (left side, Anton font)
parent.socialNav: nav > ul (right side, inner blocks container)

qualityChecklist: Child items use <li> elements inside parent <ul>
SVG icons use currentColor (no hardcoded colors)
Icon links have aria-label and sr-only text for screen readers
PHP only renders items with non-empty URL
TSX and PHP produce identical HTML structure
No hardcoded hover colors on items

parent.name: heading
parent.description: Heading text displayed on the left side (e.g., 'Follow Us'). Uses Anton font at heading-5 size (24px).

child.name: link
child.description: Link configuration with URL, title, aria-label, and new tab option. opensInNewTab defaults to true for social media links.

child.name: logoId
child.description: WordPress media attachment ID for the social media icon SVG. Not an image object -- icons don't need focal point.

child.name: label
child.description: Visible text label displayed next to the icon (e.g., 'Facebook', 'Instagram'). This is the text the user sees on the frontend, separate from the link title/aria-label. Figma shows this as Roboto Medium 12px.

section.notes: 32px margin-top (mt-8) + 1px solid charcoal border-top + 32px padding-top (pt-8). The border-t color uses charcoal since the Figma shows a dark separator line and the block is on a light background.

container.classes: container flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:items-center md:justify-between
container.notes: Mobile: stacked vertically with gap. Desktop: horizontal flex with heading on left, social links pushed right via justify-between.

heading.notes: Anton font at 24px (text-header-5 maps to 24px mobile, 28px at md breakpoint). Line-height 1.2 from typography system. Figma shows 1.15 which is very close to the theme token. Color inherits from section (charcoal/black on light backgrounds).

socialNav.notes: Nav wraps the ul of social items. shrink-0 prevents flex shrinking.

socialList.classes: flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:flex-wrap md:items-center
socialList.notes: Mobile: vertical stack (flex-col) with 16px gap (gap-4). Desktop: horizontal row (md:flex-row) with 16px gap (gap-4). Gap is 16px per Figma. flex-wrap handles overflow.

link.classes: flex items-center gap-3 py-2 no-underline! transition-colors hover:opacity-70
link.notes: Flex row with 12px gap (gap-3). Vertical padding 8px (py-2). No underline. Transition for hover effect. The hover state uses opacity since the parent section controls color context. The Figma shows icon+label side by side, vertically centered.

iconWrapper.notes: 24x24px icon container. Child selector ensures SVG/img fills the container. shrink-0 prevents icon from being squished.

labelText.notes: Figma: Roboto Medium 12px / 1.5 line-height. text-xs = 12px (0.75rem). font-medium = 500 weight. leading-normal = 1.5 line-height. The font-family is 'General Sans' from the theme (--font-sans), which serves as the body font. Roboto is the Figma design token name -- map to the theme's sans-serif body font.

mobile.behavior: Per spec: items displayed one below the other, aligned to the left. The heading stacks above the social links container. Each social link item stacks vertically (flex-col on the <ul>). All content left-aligned.

tablet.behavior: Transition to horizontal layout. Heading and social links on the same row. Social links wrap if needed.

desktop.behavior: Heading on the left, social links on the right in a single horizontal row. Flex with justify-between. Matches Figma layout exactly.

emptyState.parent: Section always renders (even without heading) as long as inner blocks exist. Heading renders conditionally based on content.
emptyState.child: PHP: only renders if link.url is not empty. TSX: always renders for editing.

editorBehavior.parent: Uses RichText for heading. Appender component for adding child items. renameBlock syncs list view name with heading.
editorBehavior.child: Custom LinkControl popover for URL editing (no inline text). MediaUpload toolbar and sidebar for icon. RichText for visible label text. renameBlock syncs with link title.

patterns.name: Link
keyboardInteractions: Enter: Executes the link and moves focus to the link target
ariaRequirements: Native <a> elements with href provide link role implicitly
aria-label on icon links for screen reader accessible names

patterns.name: Landmarks
ariaRequirements: <nav> wrapper with aria-label='Social Media Links' for navigation landmark

linkItem.ariaLabel: From link.label attribute (e.g., 'Follow us on Facebook')

srOnlyText.purpose: Provides link title to screen readers via visually hidden <span class='sr-only'>

decisions.decision: Block is a standalone section (not nested inside footer/header) with border-top separator, 32px margin-top and 32px padding-top
decisions.evidence: Spec explicitly states: 'Add a border top with 32px margin and padding top'

decisions.decision: Desktop layout uses flex with justify-between: heading on left, social links on right
decisions.evidence: Figma node 723:49698 shows heading 'Follow Us' on left, social icons+labels on right in a horizontal row

decisions.decision: Mobile layout stacks items vertically, aligned to left
decisions.evidence: Spec: 'On mobile display one item below the other, aligned to the left'

Figma design token 'Headline/H7' is Anton Regular 24px with 1.15 line-height. Theme's text-header-5 maps to 24px (mobile) with 1.2 line-height. The 0.05 line-height difference is acceptable (within tolerance).

decisions.decision: Label text uses text-xs (12px) with font-medium (500 weight)
decisions.evidence: Figma design token 'Body/Body - Small' is Roboto Medium 12px/1.5. Theme equivalent is text-xs font-medium leading-normal using the theme's sans-serif font.
Figma structural analysis: Social Links container gap is 16px

decisions.decision: Icon-to-label gap within each link item is 12px (gap-3)
decisions.evidence: Figma structural analysis: Link item gap is 12px

decisions.decision: Each link item has 8px vertical padding (py-2)
decisions.evidence: Figma structural analysis: Link item padding-y is 8px
Figma structural analysis: Icon size is 24x24px

decisions.decision: Border-top color uses border-charcoal (the theme's primary dark color)
decisions.evidence: Figma shows a dark separator line. The design uses #1f1f1d (charcoal) as the primary dark color. Using border-charcoal which matches the theme's --color-charcoal token.

decisions.decision: This is a two-level parent/child inner block structure: WideSocialMedia (parent) > WideSocialMediaItem (child)
decisions.evidence: Figma shows repeatable social link items (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, YouTube). Following the social-media block type pattern with inner blocks. The structural analysis confirms isInnerBlockContainer and isRepeatable flags.

decisions.decision: Child item has a visible 'label' attribute (separate from link.title) for the text shown next to the icon
decisions.evidence: Figma shows text labels next to each icon (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Youtube). This is different from the standard social media block which only shows icons. The label is a user-editable field separate from the link title to allow different display text vs link title/aria-label.

decisions.decision: No parent restriction -- this block is standalone, not nested inside footer/header
decisions.evidence: Spec describes this as a standalone block ('Wide Social Media'), not nested in a parent section. Unlike FooterInfoSocial which has parent: ['takt/footer-info'], this block has no parent constraint.

decisions.decision: Heading is optional. When empty, social links should still render but take full width.
decisions.evidence: The heading 'Follow Us' is editable content. Following shouldDisplay pattern for optional heading. When no heading, the flex container still works -- social links will align to the start.

decisions.decision: Anton font is NOT italic. Never add italic to Anton.
decisions.evidence: Figma token shows 'Anton Regular'. Planning agent critical rule: 'NEVER add italic to Anton font. Anton appears slanted but is NOT italic.'

variations.demoDescription: Only heading, two social items with icons and labels. Tests empty state handling for optional fields.
All fields populated with realistic content. Four social media links with icons and labels matching the Figma design.
Eight social media items to test flex wrapping behavior on smaller viewports.
Tests overflow behavior with a long heading string.

attributes.heading: Follow Us on All of Our Social Media Platforms and Stay Connected

variations.demoDescription: Tests empty heading state. Heading is omitted so shouldDisplay hides it. Social links should still render correctly.
Tests rendering with only one social media link.
